mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad economic strength and robust corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Rank Information Coefficient (IC) remains below 0.03 over any continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds a restrictive threshold of 3.0%, higher borrowing costs compress corporate margins and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and decelerating housing starts (HOUST) signals a late-cycle economy where consumer spending is strong but interest-rate sensitive sectors are weakening, portending an economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) during a post-recession recovery phase indicates a healthy rebound in the manufacturing sector, driving corporate revenue growth and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if positive 20-day returns do not follow accelerating INDPRO in at least 60% of instances during defined recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.5 percentage points over three months is a reliable leading indicator of a recession, causing investors to de-risk portfolios and driving equity prices lower.
failure_condition: The hit rate of this signal (correctly predicting negative 60d returns) falls below 55% over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high while the central bank remains passive (FEDFUNDS is stable), the Fed is 'behind the curve', increasing the risk of future stagflation or abrupt, aggressive tightening, both of which are negative for equities.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change < 0.1pp
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting SPX on triggered signals fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects fundamental economic expansion, which translates directly into higher aggregate corporate earnings and supports positive equity returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following positive QoQ GDPC1 growth announcements is not statistically different from the average return following negative announcements (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE below 4.5%) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an overheating labor market, prompting expectations of imminent monetary tightening by the Fed, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: A regression of 20-day returns on UNRATE and CPIAUCSL levels shows an insignificant or positive coefficient for their interaction term when UNRATE is below 4.5%.
